title: Basel III OPR25.8 — Business continuity and resilience: recovery objective monitoring
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=Basel III, clause=OPR25.8, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for OPR25.8.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that Basel III OPR25.8 (Business continuity and resilience) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.25: Basel III OPR25.8 — Business continuity and resilience: recovery objective monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.25' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: BCP/DR test results, failover event logs, backup verification records, critical-service availability metrics.
spl:
index=bcp_dr sourcetype IN (bcp_test_results, dr_failover, backup_verification) earliest=-90d
| eval rto_met=if(actual_recovery_time<=target_rto, 1, 0)
| eval rpo_met=if(data_loss_minutes<=target_rpo, 1, 0)
| stats count AS tests sum(rto_met) AS rto_pass sum(rpo_met) AS rpo_pass latest(test_date) AS last_test BY critical_service, test_type
| eval rto_compliance_pct=round(100*rto_pass/tests, 1)
| eval rpo_compliance_pct=round(100*rpo_pass/tests, 1)
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-strptime(last_test, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400)
| where rto_compliance_pct<100 OR rpo_compliance_pct<100 OR days_since_test>180
| table critical_service, test_type, tests, rto_compliance_pct, rpo_compliance_pct, days_since_test
description: Monitors BCP/DR test outcomes against RTO/RPO targets and flags critical services that have not been tested within 180 days, providing evidence of operational resilience per OPR25.8.
value: OPR25.8 requires banks to maintain business continuity plans with defined recovery objectives. Continuous monitoring of test results and recovery metrics provides auditable evidence that resilience controls are effective.
implementation: (1) Ingest BCP/DR test results with target_rto, target_rpo, actual_recovery_time, and data_loss_minutes; (2) schedule monthly; (3) flag services failing targets or overdue for testing. Indexes required: `index=bcp_dr`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=backup_verification`, `sourcetype=bcp_test_results`, `sourcetype=dr_failover`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `critical_service`, `days_since_test`, `last_test`, `rpo_compliance_pct`, `rpo_met`, `rpo_pass`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: BCP/DR test results, failover event logs, backup verification records, critical-service availability metrics.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest BCP/DR test results with target_rto, target_rpo, actual_recovery_time, and data_loss_minutes; (2) schedule monthly; (3) flag services failing targets or overdue for testing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=bcp_dr sourcetype IN (bcp_test_results, dr_failover, backup_verification) earliest=-90d
| eval rto_met=if(actual_recovery_time<=target_rto, 1, 0)
| eval rpo_met=if(data_loss_minutes<=target_rpo, 1, 0)
| stats count AS tests sum(rto_met) AS rto_pass sum(rpo_met) AS rpo_pass latest(test_date) AS last_test BY critical_service, test_type
| eval rto_compliance_pct=round(100*rto_pass/tests, 1)
| eval rpo_compliance_pct=round(100*rpo_pass/tests, 1)
| eval days_since_test=round((now()-strptime(last_test, "%Y-%m-%d"))/86400)
| where rto_compliance_pct<100 OR rpo_compliance_pct<100 OR days_since_test>180
| table critical_service, test_type, tests, rto_compliance_pct, rpo_compliance_pct, days_since_test
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
